sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added `dateTimeParts` column extractor — splits a "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" timestamp (e.g. report-detail's native "Last edited" column) into separate date and time synthetic columns.

Added `numberOfChars` column extractor — counts a source cell's whitespace-normalised visible-text length into a single right-aligned numeric synthetic column (e.g. an "Annotation chars" column for sorting/filtering annotations by size).
report-detail now uses both: 'Last edited' → ['Last edited date', 'Last edited time'], 'Annotation' → ['Annotation chars'] (added to `integerColumns` for right-aligned numeric sorting).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed a syntax error (missing comma) in report-detail's `integerColumns` array introduced by a manual edit, which broke the entire userscript.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed multi-row list cells' count-based '▶ N ▤' collapse toggle (Catalog#, Label, Authors, …) becoming permanently unresponsive to clicks right after it was first built — WIP.8's `_findCellListItems()` walked a cell's list up to the `<td>` checking for competing sibling text, but didn't exclude the toggle span it had itself just appended as a sibling of that list; the toggle's own glyph/count text (e.g. '▶3▤') was seen as competing prose on every subsequent call, so the very next click (or column-header/global mass-toggle) silently found zero list items and did nothing.

`_findCellListItems()` now excludes anything matching `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` (which already covers `.mb-cell-collapse-toggle` and other injected/decorative spans) from that sibling check, not just `<script>`/`<style>`/`<ul>`/`<ol>`.

Single-item list cells (e.g. a work with exactly one author) are no longer wrapped as a 'prose' cell — they render untouched with no toggle, as before this feature existed, instead of sometimes getting an incorrect 'more'/'less' toggle.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed the 'Authors' column on report-detail pages (e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/report/AnnotationsWorks) regressing to the prose-cell 'more'/'less' toggle instead of its correct count-based '▶ N ▤' list toggle. WIP.1's `:scope > ul > li` tightening assumed every script-generated multi-row column places its `<ul>` as a direct child of `<td>`, but native MusicBrainz 'Authors' markup wraps its list one level deeper (`<td><script type="application/json">…</script><div class="artist-roles-container"><ul class="artist-roles">…`), so the tightened selector no longer found it and the cell fell through to being treated as free-form prose.

Replaced the direct-child-only list lookup everywhere with a new `_findCellListItems()` that walks from a cell's first `<ul>`/`<ol>` up to the `<td>`, treating `<script>`/`<style>` tags and sibling lists as non-competing, but still correctly rejecting a wiki-rendered list embedded inside actual 'Annotation' prose text (real sibling text anywhere along that walk disqualifies it).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed the 'Annotation' column header's collapse-toggle count (`.mb-col-collapse-count`, e.g. showing '5' while its own tooltip correctly said '496') and the unique-values dropdown's 'Cell structure' collapsed/expanded counts being wrong for prose (free-text) collapsable columns. Several places independently recomputed 'is this cell multi-row?' using an unscoped `cell.querySelectorAll('ul > li')` check with no awareness of prose cells at all — it only matched cells whose Annotation text happened to contain an embedded wiki bullet/numbered list, and otherwise ignored the actual (much larger) set of collapsed prose cells entirely. Also affected — and fixed — the same way: the multi-row state column filter ('collapsed'/'expanded'/'any' entries in the unique-values dropdown actually filtering the table) and the developer stats panel's per-column multi-row count.

Added `_classifyCollapseCell()` as the single shared implementation of this check (list cells via `:scope > ul > li`, prose cells via presence of a `.mb-cell-collapse-toggle`), replacing the four independent, inconsistent implementations.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Ctrl+Click on an 'Annotation'-style cell's ▶/▼ collapse toggle now also expands (uncollapses) every nested wiki `<h2>` sub-heading inside that cell, instead of just expanding the cell's own text preview.
Ctrl+Click on the 'Annotation' column's header collapse toggle now also expands every nested wiki `<h2>` sub-heading in every cell of the column, in addition to expanding all cells.

Both toggles' tooltips now explain the Ctrl+Click behaviour — shown only when the cell/column actually contains a nested wiki sub-heading, so the hint doesn't appear on plain annotations with no headings to expand.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added `sa_annotation_h2_bg` / `sa_annotation_h2_color` settings for the text color of wiki-rendered `<h2>` sub-headings nested inside table cells (e.g. 'Annotation' column sub-sections like '== Known performances =='), separate from the existing page-level H2 section-header colors. Defaults (#e3f2fd background / #1565c0 text) are chosen to sit quietly against the grey/white alternating row backgrounds and to read as related to (rather than compete with) the standard blue hyperlink color that often appears in annotation text.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed wiki-rendered `<h2>` sub-headings inside 'Annotation'-style column cells (e.g. '== Known performances ==') becoming permanently non-collapsible after the first sort or filter. renderFinalTable()/renderGroupedTable() replace table rows with cloneNode(true) copies on every sort/filter re-render, which copies the collapsed-h2 marker classes and icon but not the click listener — makeH2sCollapsible() (which normally sets these up) only runs after the initial full-page render and a disk-load, never after a sort/filter, so the cloned headings looked processed but were inert. A new `_rewireNestedTableH2Toggles()` re-attaches working click handlers to these nested headings after every sort/filter re-render, without touching page-level h2 sections (Genres, Tags, Relationships, …) or resetting any section's manually-toggled state.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Made the collapsed-cell clamp height of 'Annotation'-style columns configurable via a new `sa_annotation_column_max_height_em` setting (default 5.6em, ~4 lines — the same value used in WIP.1), instead of a hardcoded CSS value.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Added a new '📝 ANNOTATION COLUMNS' settings section: `sa_enable_annotation_collapse` (checkbox, default on) toggles the WIP.1 free-text collapse/height-clamp behaviour on/off for 'Annotation'-style columns, and `sa_annotation_column_max_width` (number, default 480) makes the auto-resize width cap configurable.

The auto-resize width cap for these columns is now always active, independent of `sa_enable_annotation_collapse` — even with collapsing disabled, a long annotation no longer stretches the column to its unwrapped width.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Annotation-style columns declared in `collapsableColumns` (report-detail's AnnotationsPlaces/AnnotationsReleases sub-reports, the instrument 'Annotations' report category) are now collapsible: free-text cells are height-clamped behind a ▶/▼ 'show more/less' toggle, sharing the same column-header and global mass-toggle buttons, filter-match highlighting, and expand-state persistence as existing list-type collapsable columns (Catalog#, Label, …).
Auto-resize (↔️ Resize / Ctrl+R) no longer sizes these free-text columns to a paragraph's unwrapped single-line width — their measured width is now capped, matching the new height-clamped rendering.

Tightened `collapsableColumns`' list-cell detection to only match a `<ul><li>` that is a direct child of the `<td>`, fixing a latent bug where a wiki-rendered bullet/numbered list embedded inside free-text annotation prose could be mistaken for a script-generated multi-row list and partially hidden.
